Command: "# Iter 2: 30% done, 80160 violations. Slow reduction (~400-1000 per 10%).
# This is the "convergence plateau" phase - each iteration reduces violations
# by smaller and smaller amounts.
# With 80K violations and ~500/10%/iter = ~8000 per iteration reduction.
# Full convergence would need 80000/500 = ~160 more iterations (SLOW!).
# 
# BUT: TritonRoute typically accelerates in later iterations as it finds
# better solutions for the remaining violations.
# 
# At 64 max iterations and 45 min each = 48 hours for full DRT.
# However, the router may stop early if violations stop decreasing.
#
# This is expected behavior for a highly constrained 35% utilization design.
# DRT WILL complete, just needs time.
# 
# Set up sustained monitoring with 30-min polls for up to 24h.

WRAPPER_RUN="/workspace/nc-project-20260529-210526/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu/openlane/mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu_wrapper/runs/wrapper_run"
LOG="/tmp/librelane_final.log"
LR_PID=824793

for i in $(seq 1 48); do
  sleep 1800

  LR_ALIVE=$(ps -p $LR_PID -o pid --no-headers 2>/dev/null | xargs)
  ELAPSED=$(ps -p $LR_PID -o etime --no-headers 2>/dev/null | xargs)
  STEP=$(ls -t "$WRAPPER_RUN/" 2>/dev/null | grep "^[0-9]" | head -1)
  FINAL_GDS=$(ls "$WRAPPER_RUN/final/gds/"*.gds 2>/dev/null | head -1)
  DRT=$(grep "Completing\|Start [0-9]*th\|Total wire.*um\|0 DRC\|DRC passed" "$LOG" 2>/dev/null | tail -4 | tr '\n' '|' | head -c 140)
  LOG_TAIL=$(tail -1 "$LOG" 2>/dev/null | xargs | cut -c1-80)

  echo "[$(date '+%H:%M:%S')] LR=${LR_ALIVE:-DONE}(${ELAPSED}) step=$STEP"
  echo "  DRT: $DRT"
  echo "  tail: $LOG_TAIL"
  [ -n "$FINAL_GDS" ] && echo "  *** GDS: $FINAL_GDS ***"
  echo ""

  if [ -z "$LR_ALIVE" ]; then
    echo "=== LR DONE ==="
    ls -lt "$WRAPPER_RUN/" | head -40
    find "$WRAPPER_RUN/final/" 2>/dev/null | sort | head -20
    tail -60 "$LOG"
    break
  fi
done" timed out
